title: CandidateProfile
description: Workspace-private candidate profile. Single source of truth for both Discovery and FitReport pipelines. One profile per workspace (MVP). Discovery reads narrative fields via ProfileSnapshot adapter; FitReport reads all fields directly from candidate_profiles table.

profile_hash.description: MD5 of the profile content fields. Used for FitReport cache invalidation.

summary.description: 1-3 sentence professional positioning. Used as 'Background' context in FitReport LLM prompt and as the primary narrative signal for Discovery intent translation.

experience_summary.description: Broader career narrative: firms, roles, key responsibilities. Enriches Discovery search direction.

education_summary.description: Highest degree and institution. e.g. 'MS Financial Engineering, Columbia University'.

years_experience.description: Total years of relevant professional experience.

technical_skills.description: Programming languages, quantitative methods, and data tools. UI label: 'Technical skills / methods'. Covers what was previously split across technical_skills and analytical_methods. e.g. ['Python', 'SQL', 'VaR', 'Monte Carlo', 'stress testing']

domain_experience.description: Finance/risk functional domains. e.g. ['market risk', 'model validation', 'credit risk', 'PPNR']

finance_domains.description: Asset classes and product knowledge. e.g. ['derivatives', 'fixed income', 'structured products', 'equities']

tools.description: Software platforms and vendor tools. e.g. ['Bloomberg', 'Murex', 'QuantLib', 'Tableau', 'PowerBI']

representative_projects.description: Primary evidence for FitReport strong_matches and gap analysis. Each entry is cited by the LLM when writing evidence-based match assessments.

description.description: What you did and the business context. Be specific.

skills_used.description: Skills and tools directly exercised in this project.

quantified_impact.description: Optional metric or outcome. e.g. 'Reduced model run time by 40%.'
